headline: Samsung union deal averts 18-day chip strike: 10.5% profit-share in stock + 1.5% cash; shares +7.6% in Seoul
summary: Late Wed Samsung Electronics reached a tentative wage agreement with its 48,000-member union, suspending the strike that began Thursday. Deal pledges 10.5% of operating profit as stock bonuses plus 1.5% cash; targets >KRW 200T chip operating profit 2026-2028. Union vote May 22-27. Samsung shares jumped as much as 7.6% in Seoul; Kospi +6%. Removes the 3% global memory supply risk that was lifting spot DRAM prices.

headline: Memory share-gain trade reverses as Samsung strike risk lifts: MU, SK Hynix give back rally fuel
summary: With the Samsung strike suspended ahead of US open, the bullish supply-disruption case Jefferies and BofA leaned on this week loses force. Memory spot prices had been climbing — 64GB DDR5 RDIMM hit $1,350 mid-May, +11% MoM — partly on Samsung outage fears. BofA's Vivek Arya had recently bumped MU PT to $950 citing share-gain optionality; that thesis component is now weaker, though the underlying DRAM tightness (Gartner: +47% 2026 price growth) is intact.

headline: Intel and Apple in advanced foundry talks for 18A; deal would anchor Intel's external-customer pipeline
summary: WSJ/Yahoo report Apple and Intel reached a preliminary agreement for Intel to manufacture some Apple chips on Intel 18A, after >1 year of discussions. Would mark the first Tier-1 external customer for Intel Foundry beyond the US government stake (10%). INTC fell 3.24% on May 21 on profit-taking after the year's run-up. Open questions: which silicon, what volume, packaging vs. wafer, and whether the work touches leading-edge 14A.

headline: NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72 wins Computex Golden Award + Sustainable Tech award; claims 10x inference/W vs Blackwell
summary: Ahead of Jensen Huang's June 1 Taipei keynote, NVIDIA's Vera Rubin NVL72 rack platform was honored across four Computex categories. 36 Vera CPUs + 72 Rubin GPUs unified by Gen-6 NVLink Switch, ConnectX-9 SuperNICs, Spectrum-X co-packaged optics, BlueField-4 DPUs. NVIDIA touts 10x inference performance/watt and 10x lower $/token vs. prior generation — central to the FY27/FY28 capex story Goldman now models at $410B/$635B revenue.

headline: CoreWeave $3.1B DDTL 5.0 GPU-backed loan gets Moody's Ba2 / Fitch BB+; pricing tightened 50bp on oversubscription
summary: Following Mon's announcement, full deal terms posted: SOFR+450, draws to Sep 2026, final maturity Nov 2031, MS + MUFG joint-lead. Moody's Ba2 and Fitch BB+ ratings are a notch below investment grade but mark the first publicly syndicated HPC-backed financing vehicle, broadening institutional access to AI-infrastructure debt. Investor demand let pricing tighten 50bp from initial talk. Proceeds back two large non-investment-grade customer contracts.

headline: NextEra-Dominion $67B all-stock merger reshapes datacenter power map: 130 GW of large-load pipeline
summary: Mon's NEE bid for D combines the largest US clean-energy developer with the local utility for Northern Virginia datacenter alley. Combined pipeline disclosed: 130 GW of 'large-load' (read: datacenter) opportunities, ~10M utility customers across FL/VA/NC/SC. Deal expected to close mid-to-late 2027. Creates the largest regulated electric utility globally and a single counterparty for hyperscalers siting in PJM/Dominion footprint. Watch FERC and state PUC tone over the next two weeks.

headline: Siemens Energy raises 2026 outlook on datacenter pull-through; Grid Technologies orders past €7B in Q2
summary: ENR.DE Grid Technologies booked record €7B+ in Q2; total Q2 orders €17.7B with 'triple-digit percentage' growth from datacenter customers per CEO Busch. Echoes the Q1 GE Vernova print ($2.4B datacenter orders > all of 2025). Gas-turbine and HV transformer lead times now multi-year. Positive read-across to ETN, VRT, ABB and the broader US grid-equipment complex; pressures hyperscaler buildout schedules.

headline: Workday Q1 FY27 revenue $2.54B (+13.5% YoY), op margin 13.3% vs 1.8% prior year — enterprise software demand intact
summary: WDAY (not in our tracker but a key enterprise SaaS demand-tape signal) printed Q1 FY27 subscription rev $2.35B (+14.3% YoY) and operating income $338M vs $39M prior year. Positive read-through for CRM, NOW, SNOW: enterprise budgets for HR/finance/agentic-AI applications still expanding even as IT teams prioritize GPU/datacenter capex.
